layer.label: Sinai Greek NF M 48, Overtext (Greek minuscule)
Sinai Greek NF M 48, Undertext (Greek minuscule, Greek majuscule)

note: Informal minuscule, upright or sloping to the right, with numerous abbreviations. The liturgical rubrics are written in a small “Alexandrinische Auszeichnungs-Majuskel”

note.value: The text presents many variations and different arrangements compared to the editions

Bibliography: New finds of Sinai (Athens, 1999), p. 166 | For abbreviations of the editions see Mateos 1962, 1963 = J. Mateos (ed.), Le typicon de la Grande Église. ms. Saint-Croix no. 40, Xe siècle, vol. 1, Le cycle des douze mois, Orientalia Christiana Analecta 165 (Rome, 1962); vol. 2, Le cycle des fêtes mobiles, Orientalia Christiana Analecta 166 (Rome, 1963) ; Arranz 1969 = Le typicon du Monastère du Saint Sauveur à Messine, Codex Messinensis Gr. 115 A.D. 1131, introduction, texte critique, and notes by M. Arranz SJ, Orientalia Christiana Analecta 185 (Rome, 1969) ; HC = Ὡρολόγιον … κατᾶ τὴν ἔκπαλαι τάξιν οὐ μὴν ἀλλὰ καὶ τυπικὸν τοῦ τῆς Κρυπτοφέρρης μοναστηρίου (Rome, 1677) ; PKA = A. Papadopulos-Kerameus, Ἀναλέκτα Ἱεροσολυμιτικῆς Σταχυολογίας, vols. 1-4 (St. Petersburg, 1891-1898) ; TR = Τριῴδιον κατανυκτικόν (Rome, 1879)
According to the sequence of the text, it is possible to assume that the bifolios 24-25 and 22-27 belonged to the same quire
Description of undertext by Pasquale Orsini

Bibliography: New finds of Sinai (Athens, 1999), p. 166 | For abbreviations of the editions see Mateos 1962, 1963 = J. Mateos (ed.), Le typicon de la Grande Église. ms. Saint-Croix no. 40, Xe siècle, vol. 1, Le cycle des douze mois, Orientalia Christiana Analecta 165 (Rome, 1962); vol. 2, Le cycle des fêtes mobiles, Orientalia Christiana Analecta 166 (Rome, 1963) ; Arranz 1969 = Le typicon du Monastère du Saint Sauveur à Messine, Codex Messinensis Gr. 115 A.D. 1131, introduction, texte critique, and notes by M. Arranz SJ, Orientalia Christiana Analecta 185 (Rome, 1969) ; HC = Ὡρολόγιον … κατᾶ τὴν ἔκπαλαι τάξιν οὐ μὴν ἀλλὰ καὶ τυπικὸν τοῦ τῆς Κρυπτοφέρρης μοναστηρίου (Rome, 1677) ; PKA = A. Papadopulos-Kerameus, Ἀναλέκτα Ἱεροσολυμιτικῆς Σταχυολογίας, vols. 1-4 (St. Petersburg, 1891-1898) ; TR = Τριῴδιον κατανυκτικόν (Rome, 1879)

note: Script of eastern Mediterranean provenance (Ganz). The predominant characterisation is Visigothic minuscule, with characteristic use of uncial G and of tall, clubbed ascenders. There are curiously few abbreviations and no apparent use of the distinctive Visigothic abbreviation symbol consisting of a bar with a punctus above. The use of theta-shaped e in ampersand is a little odd, though, and may suggest a ninth-century date. The initial E on fol. 2r incorporates a fish of Frankish pre-Carolingian type, but combined with foliate features. The script has the characteristic (Semitic influenced?) slant of other early Latin scripts from Sinai and that, along with a rather self-conscious, forced aspect, the absence of other usual Visigothic features, and the Frankish element in the decoration would tend to suggest that the hand was working at Sinai rather than in Iberia (Brown)

note.value: Description by Michelle P. Brown, David Ganz

Bibliography: Most of the texts are edited in J. Mearns, Early Latin hymnaries: An index of hymns in hymnaries before 1100 (Cambridge, 1913) ; and A. S. Walpole, Early Latin hymns (Cambridge, 1922). Walpole discusses the Mozarabic hymnal. Text identification and description by David Ganz
All folios most probably belong to the same undertext, even though not all passages can be identified
Description of undertext by Michelle P. Brown, David Ganz

Bibliography: Most of the texts are edited in J. Mearns, Early Latin hymnaries: An index of hymns in hymnaries before 1100 (Cambridge, 1913) ; and A. S. Walpole, Early Latin hymns (Cambridge, 1922). Walpole discusses the Mozarabic hymnal. Text identification and description by David Ganz

location.repository: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai

note.value: Binding of reinforced leather from three pieces, stitched together horizontally; fastening strips
Quire signatures in outer upper margin for quire 2
